facts.context: Meiosis is a type of cell division in which germ cells divide to produce haploid cells. Where does meiosis occur?

As global temperatures increase, certain organisms will be more affected than others. The changes associated with global warming may result in an increase in sea level. Which organisms will be affected most as a result of the change in sea level?
facts.truth: fish that live in coral reefs in shallow waters
distractors: birds that eat fish from shallow waters
mammals that swim in cold, deep ocean waters
crustaceans at the bottom of deep sea ocean waters

Plants and animals are composed of organic compounds. Which of the following are the common elements found in organic compounds?
Which factor determines if two individuals are members of the same species?
facts.truth: They mate and produce fertile offspring.
distractors: They live in the same habitat and niche.
They forage and eat the same type of food.
They are similar in appearance and color.

facts.context: Most plants begin their life cycle as ___.
Which part of a plant takes in water and nutrients from the soil?
Scientists have discovered organisms that thrive in hot water that is 90°C (194°F) and do not need sunlight or oxygen to live. Into which kingdom would the scientists classify these organisms?
Animals give off carbon dioxide that plants take in through leaves. What do plants give off as a result of photosynthesis that animals need to breathe?
Which organ system communicates with cells in muscle tissue so that the muscles contract or relax, allowing the body to move?
What characteristic of carbon [C] makes it essential to living organisms?
facts.truth: Carbon bonds in many ways with itself to form chains.
distractors: Carbon forms crystal structures under certain conditions.
Carbon can exist as a solid, liquid, or gas.

facts.context: How do plants get water and minerals?
facts.truth: absorbed from the soil though the roots
distractors: absorbed from the soil through the stem
absorbed from the air through the flowers
absorbed from the air through the leaves

If the climate in an area changed over time to become very cold, which change would most likely help a species living there to survive?
facts.truth: an increase in fur and fat
distractors: a thicker neck and longer tail
a larger mouth and stronger jaws
an increase in height and speed

facts.context: When trees are removed from a tropical rain forest, other plants and animals are affected. Which statement identifies another change that will most likely occur when a rain forest habitat is destroyed?
distractors: The temperature of Earth will decrease.
The air pollution in the atmosphere will decrease.

In which way is a prokaryotic cell different from a eukaryotic cell?
facts.truth: A prokaryotic cell has no nucleus.
distractors: A prokaryotic cell can reproduce itself.
A prokaryotic cell has a cell membrane.

Animal wastes contain bacteria that make organisms sick. These wastes are often washed into water supplies by storms. How might a pet owner prevent these bacteria from polluting water supplies?
facts.truth: Bag the pet waste and throw it in the trash.
distractors: Brush the pet daily to remove bacteria.
Feed pets food that produces less waste.
Wash the pet with shampoo to kill the bacteria.

facts.context: Anne and her father find a 30-million year-old tropical plant fossil in Louisiana. Which question does the fossil help them answer?
facts.truth: What was the climate like in Louisiana 30 million years ago?
distractors: Were there mountains in Louisiana 30 million years ago?
Were there earthquakes in Louisiana 30 million years ago?
What kind of animals lived in Louisiana 30 million years ago?

Two species of garter snake in the genus Thamnophis originated from a single ancestor. These two species live in the same geographic area, however, one lives in arid land and the other lives in water.Which of the following best identifies the cause of speciation between these two garter snakes?
When taken to a new habitat, non-native plants often threaten native plants of the new habitat. Why do non-native plants threaten native plants?
facts.truth: Non-native plants compete with native plants for resources.
distractors: Non-native plants are able to mutate rapidly.
Non-native plants cause native animals to relocate.
Non-native plants are able to be used for medicine.
